title: SQL Injection
description: An attacker exploits insufficient input validation by injecting malicious SQL statements into input fields, URL parameters, or HTTP headers that are later passed to a backend database. This allows the attacker to manipulate database queries, bypass authentication mechanisms, extract sensitive data, modify or delete records, and in some cases execute operating system commands through the database engine. This is particularly dangerous in applications that construct SQL queries using string concatenation with unsanitised user input.
mitigation: Use parameterised queries and input validation.
